verification_gate: meta exec -- verify/status runs across two or more peer repos; release-please dry-run produces expected tags; install-via-profile proven from nix-profile paths.
proof_summary: Two of the three gate elements PASS. (1) `meta exec` runs across >=2 peer repos: `meta --include handoff,lifeos exec -- echo ok` returned ok for both, and `meta --include handoff,rusty-idd-unified,lifeos exec -- git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` returned real per-repo branches (handoff=develop, lifeos=main). `meta git status` additionally runs the status surface across the whole fleet. (2) install-via-profile is proven from nix-profile paths: `meta` and `git-kb` in /home/flexnetos/.nix-profile/bin are symlinks into /nix/store/...-lifeos-foundation-yzx, i.e. installed via the nix profile. The meta_mcp control surface was driven over stdio JSON-RPC: initialize returned serverInfo meta-mcp/0.1.0 and tools/list returned 29 meta_* tools (>= the 28 the row tracks; superset upgrade), including meta_exec, meta_git_status, meta_snapshot_*. The remaining element -- release-please dry-run producing expected tags -- was NOT run: release-please is not installed on this host (see blocker).

blockers.detail: release-please is not installed (`release-please` -> command not found; no npx wrapper available). The config exists at /home/flexnetos/meta/release-please-config.json and /home/flexnetos/meta/.release-please-manifest.json, but the tool to run the dry-run is absent.
blockers.owner_action: Install release-please (npm i -g release-please, or add to the nix toolchain), then run `release-please --dry-run` against the meta config and confirm the expected tags. This is read-only (dry-run) and can be closed without a commit.

live_commands.purpose: meta exec real read-only cross-repo command
live_commands.command: meta --include handoff,rusty-idd-unified,lifeos exec -- git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD
